Bug / Task Description: The sliding-window rate limiter over-counts right after a window rotation: when one window has elapsed it resets the window origin to the current instant instead of sliding it forward by exactly one window size. This zeroes the interpolation weight so the previous window contributes its full count, and legitimate requests are wrongly denied immediately after rotation. Steady-state checks that don't cross a rotation boundary still pass.
Commit Subject: fix(ratelimit): advance sliding window by one window, not to now
